task_completion.description: Agent navigates the map view to Central Park in New York City.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewport shows an aerial or map view of the large rectangular Central Park in Manhattan, New York City.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the Ruler or Path measurement tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Ruler', 'Path', or 'New Path' is visible on the screen, usually containing tabs for 'Line' or 'Path'.

task_completion.description: Agent traces the perimeter of Central Park.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored line (path) is visible outlining the rectangular border of Central Park (bounded by 59th St, 110th St, Central Park West, and 5th Ave).

task_completion.description: The path consists of at least 20 points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The path line shows numerous vertices (dots or squares) along the border, clearly exceeding 20 points, indicating a manual trace rather than a simple 4-point rectangle.

task_completion.description: The measured length is within the acceptable range (9.5 - 10.5 km).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The measurement dialog displays a distance/length value between 9.5 km and 10.5 km (or approximately 5.9 to 6.5 miles).
The name field in the path properties or save dialog contains the exact text 'Central_Park_Perimeter'.

task_completion.description: The path is saved to 'My Places'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An item named 'Central_Park_Perimeter' is visible in the 'Places' or 'My Places' sidebar/panel, indicating the task is finalized.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the mapping software GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mapping application interface (e.g., Google Earth Pro) being used directly, not a script, terminal, or text editor.

integrity.description: The path is created through interaction, not pasted.
integrity.visual_evidence: The path points appear sequentially or the path is constructed step-by-step across frames, rather than a complex shape appearing instantly.

integrity.description: The length is derived from the tool, not typed manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen typing the length value (9.6 km) into a text field; the value is displayed by the software as a result of the tracing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Central Park is a large rectangular park in Manhattan. Its verified perimeter is approximately 9.6 to 9.7 km (approx 6 miles). The boundaries are 40.764 to 40.800 North latitude and -73.981 to -73.949 West longitude.
